title: senecio neomexicanus var. toumeyi
intro: Senecio neomexicanus var. toumeyi, also known as Toumey's ragwort or Toumey's fireweed, is a rare and unique succulent plant native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal medicine, where it is used to treat a variety of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Senecio neomexicanus var. toumeyi include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing, reduce pain and swelling, and promote overall well-being. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or tincture by infusing the leaves and stems in water or a solvent, such as ethanol or glycerin, to create a medicinal liquid that can be consumed orally for its therapeutic benefits.
